name: MAS Project Orchid (Purpose-Bound Money)
chain: Multiple (DBS, OCBC, UOB, JPMorgan participants tested various)

custodian.name: Participating banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) for tokenized SGD deposits

summary: MAS's flagship retail and wholesale tokenization framework. Defines 'Purpose-Bound Money' (PBM) as a programmable wrapper around digital money (tokenized deposits, stablecoins, or future digital SGD) enabling conditional payments. Successful use cases include government vouchers (CDC vouchers via DBS), commercial vouchers, escrowed payments, and cross-border remittance corridors. Project Orchid is complemented by Project Guardian (institutional asset tokenization, with global expansion including UK FCA and Japan FSA cooperation). Defines Singapore's distinct regulatory approach: programmable money without committing to retail CBDC issuance.

sources.title: MAS: Project Guardian and global asset tokenization pilots (2024)

underlying_asset: Tokenized SGD bank deposits + future digital SGD; PBM wraps these for programmable use cases (vouchers, escrow, conditional payments).
reserve_composition: 1:1 with underlying bank deposit liability or future central bank liability.
secondary_market: Per use case; vouchers typically non-transferable by design.
redemption_mechanism: PBM converts to standard tokenized deposit upon condition fulfillment; tokenized deposit redeems 1:1 against issuing bank.
upgrade_authority: MAS framework governance; participating institutions implement.

timeline.event: Project Orchid launched at Singapore FinTech Festival
PBM whitepaper published; first government CDC voucher pilot via DBS
Phase 2 announced with cross-border use cases (Project Guardian alignment)
MAS + UK FCA + Japan FSA joint tokenization pilots announced
